---
name: intake-structuring
description: Turn vague requests, notes, chat threads, and stakeholder asks into structured intake artifacts. Use this when the input is messy and needs a problem statement, scope, assumptions, acceptance criteria, risks, and next steps.
---

Use this skill to normalize rough input into an actionable work item.

## Inputs that fit this skill
- meeting notes
- email threads
- support escalations
- stakeholder bullet points
- pasted chat or comment threads
- partially formed user stories

## Output shape
Prefer this sequence:
1. Request summary
2. Problem statement
3. Desired outcome
4. Users / stakeholders
5. In scope
6. Out of scope
7. Facts
8. Assumptions
9. Open questions
10. Acceptance criteria
11. Dependencies
12. Risks / constraints
13. Recommended next owner or next agent

## Rules
- Separate the problem from the proposed solution.
- Do not let missing details stop a good first draft.
- Capture only the open questions that materially affect correctness or scope.
- Make the result easy to hand to a manager or engineer.
